title: ws.rs: PoW replay TTL (600s) < token validity window (659s)
description: relay/src/ws.rs:321 sets replay set TTL to 600 seconds. But core/src/pow.rs allows PoW tokens where abs_diff(now_floor, ts_floor) <= 10 minute-floors. A ts_floor at the boundary (now_floor - 10) means the token is valid until now_floor reaches ts_floor + 11, which at 60s/floor is up to ts_floor*60 + 659 seconds from epoch zero — so the effective validity window is up to 659 seconds, not 600. A token used at second T is evicted from the replay set at T+600, but remains mathematically valid until T+659 (worst case). An attacker who observes a used valid token (e.g. via network capture) can replay it in the [T+600, T+659] window — approximately 59 seconds per minute boundary. Fix: increase replay TTL to 720s (12 minutes) to cover the full ±10-minute-floor window with margin.
